items.title: Bournemouth midfielder Scott rejects new contract
items.summary: Alex Scott has turned down a new Bournemouth contract amid strong interest from Arsenal, Chelsea and Manchester United, Sky Sports News understands

items.title: Fifa opens investigation into Argentina post-final trouble - what happens next?
items.summary: Leandro Paredes was shown red card after final whistle Fifa looking into Falklands banner at England game Fifa has opened an investigation into the violent scenes that followed Spain’s World Cup final win on Sunday, with several Argentina players facing bans. Nahuel Molina appeared to strike Spain’s captain Rodri in the stomach as he ran on to the pitch following the final whistle, while there was also a large melee on the halfway line, where Leandro Paredes appeared to grab Eric García by the throat before throwing Gavi to the ground.

items.title: Chelsea step up interest in Palace's Lacroix

items.summary: Former England captain and manager Kevin Keegan dies at the age of 75, seven weeks after he revealed he had been diagnosed with stage four cancer

items.summary: This year's World Cup is finally over - and after 48 teams took part, it was a long, long tournament. But just as we bid to say goodbye to the 2026 edition, this competition may be getting even bigger

items.title: Spurs break their women’s transfer record to sign Alice Sombath from Lyonnes
items.summary: France defender is club’s sixth signing of the summer Coach Ho says she is aggressive and good on the ball Tottenham have signed the France defender Alice Sombath from Lyonnes for a club record fee. The price paid for the 22-year-old has not been disclosed but the previous club record, paid for Signe Gaupset in January, was reportedly more than £375,000. “The league is very competitive, every game is a tough game and that’s what I’m most excited about,” said Sombath.

items.title: Nico Williams, Ferran Torres and Spain’s adaptable World Cup excellence | Jeff Rueter
items.summary: The two substitutes combined on the World Cup winning goal not by changing the game, but by offering it what it needed, when it was needed As Spain built into the second half of extra-time of Sunday’s World Cup final, it was already clear that Argentina hoped to make them suffer. Every challenge left no stud unshown. Their defenders labored to prevent striker Mikel Oyarzabal from his typical lanes of service, with Spain taking eleven of their 20 attempts from beyond the box.

items.title: A pioneer and a superstar, Kevin Keegan’s achievements were extraordinary
items.summary: The former England manager may be remembered by many for his ‘I’d love it’ rant in 1996, but that should not obscure the remarkable nature of his football career Kevin Keegan, who has died aged 75 , is the only British player to win the Ballon d’Or twice, was the star of Liverpool’s first European Cup-winning team, a messianic figure at Newcastle United as a player and coach, forging a team so memorable that Fifa’s president wrote to commend him, and was the captain and then manager of the England men’s national team. And yet, as Keegan would ruefully reflect, he will principally be remembered by many for an emotional outburst that precipitated Newcastle’s historic failure in a Premier League title race, a TV interview which presaged the coming era of the modern game. Keegan’s “I’d love it” rant of April 1996, directed at Alex Ferguson, b

items.title: Melvine Malard hungry for trophies after joining Chelsea from Manchester United
items.summary: United get club-record fee of about £850,000 for forward Malard follows McCabe and Matsukubo into Chelsea Chelsea have announced the signing of the France forward Melvine Malard from Manchester United on a four-year contract. The Guardian revealed last week that a deal was close for the 26-year-old, who has left United after three seasons for about £850,000. Malard had one year on her contract and United were keen to keep the forward.

items.title: João Gomes seals £38m Aston Villa move, Hall and Robinson on Manchester United list
items.summary: Brazil midfielder Gomes joins from Wolves United sign young winger Thompson from Spurs The Brazil international João Gomes has joined Aston Villa in a £38m deal. The 25-year-old midfielder, who has 10 caps, ends a three-and-a-half-year spell at Wolves, for whom he made 130 appearances after signing from Flamengo. Gomes’s arrival at Villa follows the departure of Youri Tielemans to Manchester United and comes with Morgan Rogers on the verge of a £117m move to Chelsea .

items.title: Football Daily | Give it up for Vozinha, Spence and the 2026 World Cup’s cult heroes
items.summary: Every World Cup produces cult heroes, icons who make it possible for us to sit back and start misty-eyed recollections by saying: “Do you remember Roger Milla/Saeed Al-Owairan/Keisuke Honda/Paul the Octopus?” Well, the Geopolitics World Cup has not let us down! Here are 10 cult heroes who’ve emerged from this tournament.

items.title: Fifa gives fraud ‘an open door’ with betting, says Council of Europe chief
items.summary: Fifa deal with prediction market company under fire Balogun reprieve showed ‘rules bend under pressure’ Fifa has been accused of providing an “open door to fraud” and allowing political influence to cast doubt on the integrity of the World Cup in a stinging rebuke by the Council of Europe’s secretary general. In an open letter published to coincide with Sunday’s final, Alain Berset also called for a new integrity framework to be built before the 2030 tournament, which is mainly being staged in Europe, and warned that Fifa was embroiled in a crisis involving money and power.

items.title: ‘Where did they go?’: homeless people feel force of America’s brutality in World Cup clean-up
items.summary: Fifa says football brings the world together but the unhoused in Atlanta feel targeted and completely excluded from the tournament “A lot of our community has been pushed out by the World Cup. We’re not just dollar signs, we’re more than that. We’re people and we’re frustrated that they’ve chosen to treat us less than human.” “ They dropped me off there in the middle of the night.

items.title: How are London City Lionesses able to embark on such a transfer spree?
items.summary: Many are in awe of the investment that Michele Kang is making into the women’s game. Others, though, are baffled There is one team whose transfer activity this summer has captured everyone’s attention. London City Lionesses, a club who finished in the bottom half of the second tier a little over two years ago, have stunned the women’s game by signing a flurry of big names, including the Spain stars Alexia Putellas and Mapi León, the former England goalkeeper Mary Earps, and then, on Wednesday morning, the France winger Kadidiatou Diani.

items.title: Inför Sandviken hemma: Ska IFK inleda en ny segersvit
items.summary: Nu är sommaruppehållet äntligen slut och den riktiga fotbollen börjar igen. Huvudrätten under söndagen blir matchen på Parken mot Stålmännen från Sandviken. Bruksorten är platsen där vår tränare Eldars tillbringat större delen av sitt liv, och där han blev formad som tränare.

items.title: Krönika: Mäster Skräddare
items.summary: Denna artikel är byggd kring den klassiska historien ”Mäster Skräddare”, där en man går till skräddaren för att få en rock sydd. Det blir dock ingen rock, utan för varje vecka han kommer dit så blir plagget mindre och mindre, tills det inte blir något alls. Den artikel skall läsas med STOR ironi, då den inte är menad att skada någon på något sätt utan bara få er läsare att tänka till och kanske, eventuellt, få ett gott skratt

items.title: Gaza celebrates World Cup victory of Spanish team they took into their hearts
items.summary: People gather at cafes and outdoor screenings to support team after Spain’s backing for Palestinian rights The Spanish football team’s World Cup win has been celebrated in the streets of Gaza, where Spain’s support for Palestinian rights and Israel’s backing for the Argentinian team turned the competition into more than a football showdown. Gathered at cafes and outdoor screenings, Palestinians defied the risk of Israeli attacks to cheer on a team they had taken to their hearts.

Omgångens besvikelse är regerande mästarna Mjällby som har förtvivlat svårt att vinna och som riskerar att tappa ännu mer mark i tabellen nu när Europaspelet inleds

items.title: Uppgifter: Boudri kan bli spelklar på torsdag
items.summary: Amin Borudi kopplas ihop med en flytt till Hammarby. Nu skriver Sportbladet att 21-åringen presenteras imorgon. Hammarby försökter till och med få honom spelklar till Europakvalet på torsdag

items.title: Fem spaningar efter Örgryte IS - Djurgårdens IF
items.summary: Efter två raka segrar efter det allsvenska sommaruppehållet reser blåränderna ner till västkusten för ett möte med Örgryte. I den senaste matchen mot Halmstad på hemmaplan övertygade Djurgården genom att komfortabelt vinna med 3-0 utan att aldrig riktigt vara hotade av poängtapp. Örgryte ligger näst sist i tabellen med tre poäng fler inkasserade än just Halmstad vilket innebär att Stockholms stolthet återigen är storfavorit inför matchen.
